library_preparation_preflight.summary: The artifact is a Cloud Foundry OpenTelemetry resource detector; meaningful public/provider coverage needs a Cloud Foundry-style VCAP_APPLICATION value and the OpenTelemetry autoconfigure/incubator APIs that the published artifact uses only as compileOnly/test dependencies upstream.

deterministic_setup.reason: Normal integration is through OpenTelemetry SDK autoconfiguration and the artifact publishes ResourceProvider service entries whose SPI classes are not transitive from the library itself.

The CloudFoundryResourceDetector ComponentProvider path has a method signature using DeclarativeConfigProperties from the incubator API, which is compileOnly upstream and absent from the resolved library dependencies.

library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: Set VCAP_APPLICATION for tests that exercise CloudFoundryResource.get(), ResourceProvider, ServiceLoader, or autoconfiguration. Use a JSON object with fields such as application_id, application_name, instance_index, organization_id, organization_name, process_id, process_type, space_id, and space_name. Ensure the variable is present before CloudFoundryResource is first loaded because the public singleton is initialized from System.getenv at class initialization. Parser edge cases can alternatively be tested from the same package via buildResource(Function<String,String>).
risks: Without VCAP_APPLICATION, the public detector returns Resource.empty(), so generated tests may compile but provide little meaningful coverage.
Changing VCAP_APPLICATION after CloudFoundryResource has been loaded will not affect CloudFoundryResource.get() because the resource is cached in a static singleton.

Declarative ComponentProvider coverage may require additional OpenTelemetry declarative-config test dependencies if the agent chooses to test the full declarative configuration engine rather than instantiate the detector directly.
